description_long: This is a vintage, red triplane with three stacked wings and a wooden propeller at the front, reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter aircraft. The fuselage is slender and tapers to a pointed tail with a large vertical stabilizer and horizontal tailplanes. Distinctive black-and-white cross insignias are painted on the wings and fuselage, indicating a historical military origin. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield and visible seat. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and exposed. The surface appears to be primarily wood with some metal fittings, and the overall finish is matte with visible wood grain texture.
description: A red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, black-and-white cross insignias, and an open cockpit behind a wooden propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three stacked wings, wooden propeller, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, wing arrangement, and cross insignias on the wings and body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the pointed tail, large vertical stabilizer, and the triplane's symmetrical wing structure.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the exposed landing gear and the open cockpit's position.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal tailplanes.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
